title: lemurangis decaryana
intro: Lemurangis decaryana, also known as the Philippine forest mint, is a plant species that belongs to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help in treating various health issues. Lemurangis decaryana has been traditionally used to treat fever, headache, and respiratory problems, and is also known for its ability to promote digestion and relieve nausea. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
